initial_program:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(R) :- n_rows(N), R = 0..N-1.
col(C) :- n_cols(M), C = 0..M-1.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Guess the two distinct nonzero cells (R1,C1,V1) and (R2,C2,V2)
1 { R1, C1, V1, R2, C2, V2 : input(R1,C1,V1), input(R2,C2,V2),
       V1 != V2, R1 < R2, C1 < C2 } 1 :- true.

% Orientation facts
rowPattern1 :- (R2 - R1) > 0, (R2 - R1) < (C2 - C1).
rowPattern0 :- (C2 - C1) = 0, (R2 - R1) > 0.
colPattern1 :- (C2 - C1) > 0, (C2 - C1) < (R2 - R1).
colPattern0 :- (R2 - R1) = 0, (C2 - C1) > 0.

% Placement conditions
condV1_row(R) :- (R - R1) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_row(R) :- (R - R2) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV1_col(C) :- (C - C1) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_col(C) :- (C - C2) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,V1) :- rowPattern1, condV1_row(R), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern1, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern0, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.

allowed(R,C,V2) :- rowPattern1, condV2_row(R), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern1, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern0, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.

allowed(R,C,0) :- rowPattern0, not condV1_row(R), not condV2_row(R), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,0) :- colPattern0, not condV1_col(C), not condV2_col(C), Color = 0.

% Choose exactly one colour for each output cell, respecting the allowed predicate
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- allowed(R, C, Col), output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(R) :- n_rows(N), R = 0..N-1.
col(C) :- n_cols(M), C = 0..M-1.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Guess the two distinct nonzero cells (R1,C1,V1) and (R2,C2,V2)
1 { cell(R1,C1,V1,R2,C2,V2) : input(R1,C1,V1), input(R2,C2,V2), V1 != V2, R1 < R2, C1 < C2 } 1 :- true.

% Orientation facts
rowPattern1 :- (R2 - R1) > 0, (R2 - R1) < (C2 - C1).
rowPattern0 :- (C2 - C1) = 0, (R2 - R1) > 0.
colPattern1 :- (C2 - C1) > 0, (C2 - C1) < (R2 - R1).
colPattern0 :- (R2 - R1) = 0, (C2 - C1) > 0.

% Placement conditions
condV1_row(R) :- (R - R1) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_row(R) :- (R - R2) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV1_col(C) :- (C - C1) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_col(C) :- (C - C2) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,V1) :- rowPattern1, condV1_row(R), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern1, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern0, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.

allowed(R,C,V2) :- rowPattern1, condV2_row(R), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern1, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern0, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.

allowed(R,C,0) :- rowPattern0, not condV1_row(R), not condV2_row(R), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,0) :- colPattern0, not condV1_col(C), not condV2_col(C), Color = 0.

% Choose exactly one colour for each output cell, respecting the allowed predicate
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- allowed(R, C, Col), output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

rounds.thinking:
We need to fix unsafe variable errors. The unsafe variables appear in constraints where they are only used inside aggregates or comparisons without being positively bound. We need to ensure each variable used is positively grounded before aggregates.

Common pattern: In rules like rowPattern1 :- (R2 - R1) > 0, (R2 - R1) < (C2 - C1). Variables R2,R1,C1,C2 appear only in arithmetic and comparisons, not positively grounded elsewhere. They are used as constants in other constraints but not bound. We need to add a rule that defines these variables, e.g., define them as bound to input cells: R1, C1, V1, R2, C2, V2 are input cells. Already we have input(R1,C1,V1), input(R2,C2,V2) in the choice rule; they are in the set of selected distinct nonzero cells. However, those variables are only used inside the choice (they appear in the head as part of tuple). In Clingo, the variables in the choice head are positive? Actually they are used in the head (selected tuple) and appear in the body? The choice rule selects a set of atoms; the variables are bound to the values from the chosen atoms, but they also appear in other constraints. However the error says unsafe variables in rowPattern1: R2,R1,C1,C2. The issue may be that rowPattern1, rowPattern0, etc. are used with variables that are not grounded. rounds.program_after:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(R) :- n_rows(N), R = 0..N-1.
col(C) :- n_cols(M), C = 0..M-1.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Guess the two distinct nonzero cells (R1,C1,V1) and (R2,C2,V2)
1 { cell(R1,C1,V1,R2,C2,V2) : input(R1,C1,V1), input(R2,C2,V2), V1 != V2, R1 < R2, C1 < C2 } 1 :- true.

% Orientation facts
rowPattern1 :- (R2 - R1) > 0, (R2 - R1) < (C2 - C1).
rowPattern0 :- (C2 - C1) = 0, (R2 - R1) > 0.
colPattern1 :- (C2 - C1) > 0, (C2 - C1) < (R2 - R1).
colPattern0 :- (R2 - R1) = 0, (C2 - C1) > 0.

% Placement conditions
condV1_row(R) :- (R - R1) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_row(R) :- (R - R2) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV1_col(C) :- (C - C1) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_col(C) :- (C - C2) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,V1) :- rowPattern1, condV1_row(R), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern1, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern0, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.

allowed(R,C,V2) :- rowPattern1, condV2_row(R), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern1, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern0, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.

allowed(R,C,0) :- rowPattern0, not condV1_row(R), not condV2_row(R), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,0) :- colPattern0, not condV1_col(C), not condV2_col(C), Color = 0.

% Choose exactly one colour for each output cell, respecting the allowed predicate
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- allowed(R, C, Col), output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Determine the number of rows and columns
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(R) :- n_rows(N), R = 0..N-1.
col(C) :- n_cols(M), C = 0..M-1.
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Guess the two distinct nonzero cells (R1,C1,V1) and (R2,C2,V2)
1 { cell(R1,C1,V1,R2,C2,V2) : input(R1,C1,V1), input(R2,C2,V2), V1 != V2, R1 < R2, C1 < C2 } 1 :- true.

% Orientation facts
rowPattern1 :- (R2 - R1) > 0, (R2 - R1) < (C2 - C1).
rowPattern0 :- (C2 - C1) = 0, (R2 - R1) > 0.
colPattern1 :- (C2 - C1) > 0, (C2 - C1) < (R2 - R1).
colPattern0 :- (R2 - R1) = 0, (C2 - C1) > 0.

% Placement conditions
condV1_row(R) :- (R - R1) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_row(R) :- (R - R2) \ ((R2 - R1) * 2) = 0.
condV1_col(C) :- (C - C1) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.
condV2_col(C) :- (C - C2) \ ((C2 - C1) * 2) = 0.

% Allowed colours for each cell
allowed(R,C,V1) :- rowPattern1, condV1_row(R), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern1, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.
allowed(R,C,V1) :- colPattern0, condV1_col(C), Color = V1.

allowed(R,C,V2) :- rowPattern1, condV2_row(R), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern1, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.
allowed(R,C,V2) :- colPattern0, condV2_col(C), Color = V2.

allowed(R,C,0) :- rowPattern0, not condV1_row(R), not condV2_row(R), Color = 0.
allowed(R,C,0) :- colPattern0, not condV1_col(C), not condV2_col(C), Color = 0.

% Choose exactly one colour for each output cell, respecting the allowed predicate
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- allowed(R, C, Col), output_cell(R, C).

#show output/3.
